failure_modes.text: Boundaries fail when they are drawn to protect comfort, politics, or convenience instead of the actual purpose of the work.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger When Boundaries Protect Comfort Instead of Purpose
failure_modes.mitigation: Audit whether boundaries serve the work or protect comfort: ask whether moving the boundary would improve the outcome even if it creates discomfort.

failure_modes.text: Premature narrowing can kill options and block the generation of better explanations or solutions.
failure_modes.mitigation: Delay narrowing until at least two alternative framings have been explored; premature closure is more expensive than the extra search time.

failure_modes.text: Boundary discipline breaks when familiar frameworks are applied across situations that require jumping the existing frame.
failure_modes.source_quote: failure to jump boundaries and instead apply an existing model
failure_modes.mitigation: Test the boundary against a novel frame from a different discipline; if the familiar framework cannot accommodate the new case, the boundary is misplaced.

premortem_questions.text: What is this work actually supposed to cover, and what is deliberately out of scope?
Are these limits serving the goal, or are they mainly protecting us from difficult evidence or accountability?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger When Boundaries Protect Comfort Instead of Purpose

premortem_questions.text: What important option disappears if we narrow the frame right now?

heuristics.text: Formalize the scope and expected output before the analysis sprawls.
Use boundaries to respect real cognitive limits by breaking work into genuinely manageable chunks.
Keep boundaries purpose-aligned and revisit them when they start protecting convenience instead of clarity.

curation_notes.summary: The source is naturally chunk-ready on scope discipline, anti-premature narrowing, and purpose-aligned limit setting.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor interpersonal-boundary sprawl because the raw source is stronger on analysis contracts, cognitive limits, and anti-comfort scoping.
open_questions: The third heuristic compresses several source warnings about misused limits so the model stays operational rather than rhetorical.

allies: The raw ally surface is strongest on cognitive-load-theory, decomposition, prioritization, and scaffolding-like chunking as later relation material.
antagonists: The raw antagonist pressure is strongest on law-of-the-instrument style frame fixation, but only current-vocabulary ids should survive Wave 3.
structured_tensions: The source is tension-real around comfort-protecting scope lines, but it does not expose a cleaner explicit current-vocabulary tension block than the one-hop relation surface.
